channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University detained, video shows masked people handcuffing her
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A Turkish national and doctoral student at Tufts University has been detained by U.S. Department of Homeland Security agents without explanation, her lawyer said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, KATHY McCORMACK and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Washington researchers warn of serious decline in honey bee colonies in 2025
item.description: Losses of commercial honey bee colonies are projected to be the highest on record in the last decade, according to Washington State University researchers.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Deml, KREX 5

item.title: Million-dollar lottery ticket was nearly thrown out, winner says
item.description: “I was actually kind of going to throw them away, because I said ‘I’ve never won the lottery,’” said Joaquin Vaughn Redo.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KREX 5

item.title: Palestinians protest Hamas in a rare public show of dissent in Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Thousands of Palestinians marched between the wreckage of a heavily destroyed town in northern Gaza on Wednesday in the second day of anti-war protests, with many chanting against Hamas in a rare display of public anger against the militant group.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, FATMA KHALED and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: More evacuations as wildfires burn in the Carolinas. Forecasts aren't encouraging for firefighters
item.description: More people have been asked to join the hundreds told to leave their homes in the North Carolina and South Carolina mountains as wildfires spread and the forecast for the rest of the week is not encouraging — dry and windy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Homeland Security Secretary visits El Salvador prison where deported Venezuelans are held
item.description: TECOLUCA, El Salvador (AP) — U.S. Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem on Wednesday visited the high-security El Salvador prison where Venezuelans who the Trump administration alleges are gang members have been held since their removal from the United States.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and ALEX BRANDON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: NATO clarifies comments that four missing US soldiers had died during training in Lithuania
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — NATO on Wednesday clarified comments that Secretary-General Mark Rutte made earlier in the day, when he suggested that four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania had died, even though the U.S. Army said their fate was not yet confirmed.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Justice Department goes to bat for Trump in hush money case
item.description: The Justice Department went to bat for President Trump in his hush money criminal case Wednesday, urging for the case to be moved to federal court so he can attempt to toss his guilty verdict on presidential immunity grounds.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday on Capitol Hill over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Chewing gum releases thousands of microplastics into body, study finds
item.description: A recent study found that chewing gum can release up to thousands of microplastics per piece and potentially be ingested, according to researchers.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NTSB chairwoman says reviewing the data after midair crash may prevent the next aviation accident
item.description: Someone should have spotted the alarming number of near misses in the skies over the nation's capital before the fatal midair collision that killed 67 people in January, and reviewing the data now could prevent future crashes, according to the head of the agency investigating the crash.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NATO issues new statement on fate of 4 missing Fort Stewart soldiers
item.description: WIESBADEN, Germany (WSAV) -- The NATO Acting spokesperson said that the Secretary General was referring to news articles and did not confirm the death of four U.S. Army 3rd Infantry Division soldiers reported missing in Lithuania Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, WFLA, NBC

item.title: San Diego passes first-in-the-nation policy to ban digital-only coupons at grocery stores
item.description: The San Diego City Council has passed the Grocery Pricing Transparency Ordinance, requiring grocers to offer both digital and paper coupons to all customers, promoting equal access to grocery savings opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Missing US soldiers in Lithuania killed: NATO chief
item.description: Four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania have died, NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said Wednesday. Speaking to reporters during a trip to Warsaw, Rutte said he had received word of the deaths of the four American troops.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Babysitter checking for monsters finds man hiding under child's bed in Kansas: Sheriff
item.description: The child reportedly told the babysitter there were monsters under the bed, the sheriff's office said.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Street dog rescue saves more than 1,000 Texas animals
item.description: "I literally cry with every single dog, because I watch these dogs that have been so abused and so neglected, and they learn to be part of a family," Sasha Aghili said.
item.dc:creator: Avery Travis, NewsNation

item.title: Stepmother accused of holding Connecticut man captive for over 20 years appears in court
item.description: Kimberly Sullivan arrived at Waterbury Superior Court just before 10 a.m., where she was expected to plead not guilty to kidnapping and assault charges, according to her lawyers. But the case was continued until Friday.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, Fox 5

item.title: North Carolina minimum wage could increase to $22 if bill is approved
item.description: North Carolina lawmakers will consider increasing minimum wage this session after two bills were filed proposing higher wages.
item.dc:creator: Judith Retana, NewsNation

item.title: Atlantic publishes Trump Cabinet group chat messages
item.description: The Atlantic has published the Signal group chat messages among national security leaders that were inadvertently shared with Editor-in-Chief Jeffrey Goldberg, noting administration officials said Tuesday they were not classified.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Michael Kratsios to lead White House science, tech office
item.description: Kratsios, who was Trump’s chief technology officer during his first White House term, was approved Tuesday by the upper chamber in a 74-25 vote.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Slim chance for northern lights in some states due to 'high speed stream' from Sun
item.description: Some across the U.S. may again have a chance to see the northern lights Tuesday night as material hurled out by the sun reaches Earth.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Wealthy coastal enclave nanny charged with manslaughter in toddler's death
item.description: Aimee Cotton, a babysitter on Martha's Vineyard, allegedly left small children unattended in her vehicle for three hours, leading to the death of one child.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man, 2 juveniles rescued from plane wreckage hours after crashing in Alaska
item.description: A man and two juveniles were rescued by the Alaska Army National Guard hours after crashing near Tustumena Lake near Homer, Alaska, this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: President Trump pardons former Hunter Biden business partner Devon Archer
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday pardoned a former business partner of Hunter Biden who was convicted of participating in a conspiracy to defraud a Native American tribe.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Illegal immigrant in Texas who purchased thousands of rounds for Mexican drug cartel gets 5 years in prison
item.description: An illegal immigrant in Texas who purchased thousands of rounds of ammunition for a Mexican drug cartel was sentenced to more than five years in prison.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Student returns lost backpack filled with luxury jewelry, diamonds, cash
item.description: Amid the fog of international travel, a group of Jewish students studying in Jerusalem was handed a backpack -- that none recognized as their own.
item.dc:creator: Anthony DiLorenzo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Consider deleting your 23andMe data now, California AG urges
item.description: The genetic testing company has the genetic data of more than15 million customers — and California Attorney General Rob Bonta has advised users to purge their data.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: Jonas Brothers to record new theme song for Disneyland's 70th anniversary
item.description: The Jonas Brothers are bringing a bit of Disney magic to their setlist. On Sunday, the “Happiest Place on Earth” announced that the trio will record an upbeat new theme song to celebrate the resort’s upcoming 70th anniversary.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Postmaster General Louis DeJoy resigns after 5 years in the position
item.description: The head of the beleaguered U.S. Postal Service, Louis DeJoy, resigned Monday after nearly five years in the position, leaving as President Donald Trump and Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency have floated the idea of privatizing mail service.
item.dc:creator: LISA BAUMANN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Columbia student sues Trump admin over efforts to deport her
item.description: Columbia University student Yunseo Chung is suing the Trump administration amid its efforts to deport the 21-year-old, who has been in the U.S. since she was 7 and has lawful permanent resident status.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Trump administration asks Supreme Court to halt judge's order to rehire probationary federal workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration asked the Supreme Court on Monday to halt a ruling ordering the rehiring of thousands of federal workers let go in mass firings aimed at dramatically downsizing the federal government.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he knows nothing of Atlantic journalist swept up in war plan text chain
item.description: President Trump said Monday he knew nothing of a report by a journalist for The Atlantic who says he was swept up in a text message chain with defense and national security officials while they detailed plans for an attack on Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Atlantic journalist says Trump officials included him in war plans group chat
item.description: Jeffrey Goldberg, editor in chief of The Atlantic, was apparently included in a Trump administration group chat on Signal in which top officials debated and then discussed details of attacks against Houthi rebels in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration asks Supreme Court to intervene in rehiring of federal workers
item.description: The Trump administration Monday asked the Supreme Court to block an order forcing the government to rehire thousands of workers fired as part of President Trump’s effort to drastically reduce the size of the federal workforce. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Judge won't lift block on Trump use of Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge James Boasberg declined to lift a restraining order barring the Trump administration from using the Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans, determining migrants are likely to succeed in pushing for hearings on whether they are in a gang.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: 23andMe files for bankruptcy amid financial struggles, CEO resignation
item.description: 23andMe has filed for bankruptcy and will be seeking to sell the ancestry company, a move that comes a month after it agreed to a settlement related to a 2023 data breach.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, ABC 4

item.title: Woman killed during conjugal visit with convicted California murderer, officials say
item.description: A notorious killer serving life in prison without the possibility of parole for the murder of four men in Los Angeles County reportedly strangled his wife to death during a conjugal visit late last year, according to multiple media reports.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WGN 9
